messages.content: You are a senior HVAC controls engineer extracting official ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge from a focused chapter/section. Use the provided standard context pack for definitions, cross-references, and shared control philosophy, but extract candidates only from the focus section text. Treat chunks only as evidence anchors, not extraction boundaries. FOCUS SCOPE: Extract ONLY knowledge whose primary section_id equals one of these sections or starts with one of these section prefixes followed by a dot: 5.11. Use the rest of the manual only as context for definitions, cross-references, and terminology. Do not return candidates from outside the focus scope. Evidence quotes must come from the focus scope text, not from unrelated sections used only as context. Allowed knowledge_type values: operational_sequence, parameter_spec, fault_diagnostic_rule, commissioning_step, application_guidance. Prefer high-value operational knowledge: control sequences, enable/disable logic, reset logic, staging logic, setpoints, timers, alarm/fault diagnostic rules, commissioning/TAB checks, and application rules. Do not extract front matter, legal text, table-of-contents lines, bibliography, generic definitions without operational use, or unsupported paraphrases. If the same knowledge appears multiple times, return it once with the clearest authoritative evidence_quote. evidence_quote MUST be a verbatim contiguous substring copied from the manual and it MUST support every important fact in summary, trigger_condition, required_behavior, configurable_values, fault_condition, or commissioning_check. For operational_sequence, quote the complete rule sentence or table row that includes the condition and commanded behavior. For fault_diagnostic_rule, quote the alarm or fault condition itself. For commissioning_step, quote the check or verification action. A section heading alone, a half sentence, an editor instruction such as retain/delete this section, or an introductory phrase is NOT sufficient evidence. Do not use ellipses. Do not combine non-contiguous bullets. If one exact quote cannot support the candidate, make the candidate narrower or skip it. If a requirement has alternatives, split them into separate candidates unless one exact quote supports the combined item. Return at most 60 candidates. Prefer fewer high-confidence candidates with complete evidence over broad coverage with weak evidence. Keep evidence_quote concise, usually one complete sentence or one complete table row, not a paragraph. Preserve section_id and page_hint from nearby headings and [[page=...]] anchors. Return strict JSON with fields candidates and skipped_reason only.
[redacted: ASHRAE section text omitted from artifact]

message.content:
{
  "candidates": [
    {
      "knowledge_type": "operational_sequence",
      "title": "Dual-Duct VAV Zone Control Logic with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors",
      "section_id": "5.11.5.1",
      "section_title": "Temperature and Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors",
      "equipment_scope": "Dual-duct VAV terminal unit with dual inlet airflow sensors",
      "summary": "When Zone State is cooling, cooling-loop output resets active cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum; cold duct damper modulates to maintain setpoint; hot duct damper closed. When Zone State is deadband, active cold and hot duct airflow setpoints are their last setpoints before entering deadband. When Zone State is heating, heating-loop output resets active hot duct airflow setpoint from minimum to heating maximum; hot duct damper modulates to maintain setpoint; cold duct damper closed.",
      "trigger_condition": "Zone State changes to cooling, deadband, or heating.",
      "required_behavior": "Cooling: cold duct airflow setpoint reset from minimum to cooling maximum, cold damper modulates, hot damper closed. Deadband: setpoints held at last values (minimum for the duct that was active, zero for the other). Heating: hot duct airflow setpoint reset from minimum to heating maximum, hot damper modulates, cold damper closed.",
      "configurable_values": ["Vmin", "Vcool-max", "Vheat-max"],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "a. When the Zone State is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall reset the active cold duct airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint. The cold duct damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured cooling airflow at the active cold duct airflow setpoint. The hot duct damper shall be closed. ... b. When the Zone State is deadband, the active cold duct and hot duct airflow setpoints shall be their last setpoints just before entering deadband. ... c. When the Zone State is heating, the heating-loop output shall reset the active hot duct airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint. The hot duct damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured heating airflow at the active hot duct airflow setpoint. The cold duct damper shall be closed.",
      "page_hint": 97,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "knowledge_type": "operational_sequence",
      "title": "Dual-Duct VAV Zone Control Logic with Single Discharge Airflow Sensor",
      "section_id": "5.11.5.2",
      "section_title": "Temperature and Damper Control with a Single Discharge Airflow Sensor",
      "equipment_scope": "Dual-duct VAV terminal unit with single discharge airflow sensor",
      "summary": "When Zone State is cooling, cooling-loop output resets active discharge airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum; cold duct damper modulates to maintain setpoint; hot duct damper closed. When Zone State is deadband, active discharge airflow setpoint is minimum endpoint, maintained by the damper that was operative just before entering deadband; other damper closed. When Zone State is heating, heating-loop output resets active discharge airflow setpoint from minimum to heating maximum; hot duct damper modulates to maintain setpoint; cooling damper closed.",
      "trigger_condition": "Zone State changes to cooling, deadband, or heating.",
      "required_behavior": "Cooling: discharge airflow setpoint reset from minimum to cooling maximum, cold damper modulates, hot damper closed. Deadband: discharge setpoint at minimum, maintained by previously active damper (cold if from cooling, hot if from heating), other damper closed. Heating: discharge airflow setpoint reset from minimum to heating maximum, hot damper modulates, cooling damper closed.",
      "configurable_values": ["Vmin", "Vcool-max", "Vheat-max"],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "a. When the Zone State is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall reset the active discharge airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint. The cold duct damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured discharge airflow at active discharge airflow setpoint. The hot duct damper shall be closed. ... b. When the Zone State is deadband, the active discharge airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint, maintained by the damper that was operative just before entering deadband. The other damper shall remain closed. ... c. When the Zone State is heating, the heating-loop output shall reset the active discharge airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint. The hot duct damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured discharge airflow at the active discharge airflow setpoint. The cooling damper shall be closed.",
      "page_hint": 98,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {

      "title": "Cold-Deck Supply Air Temperature Override for Cooling Mode",
      "section_id": "5.11.5.1",
      "section_title": "Temperature and Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors",
      "equipment_scope": "Dual-duct VAV terminal unit with dual inlet airflow sensors",
      "summary": "If cold-deck supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active cold duct airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.",
      "trigger_condition": "Zone State is cooling and cold-deck supply air temperature > room temperature.",
      "required_behavior": "Limit active cold duct airflow setpoint to no higher than the minimum endpoint.",
      "configurable_values": ["Vmin"],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "1. If cold-deck supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active cold duct airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.",
      "page_hint": 97,

      "section_id": "5.11.5.1",
      "section_title": "Temperature and Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors",
      "equipment_scope": "Dual-duct VAV terminal unit with dual inlet airflow sensors",

      "section_id": "5.11.5.3",
      "section_title": "Overriding Sections 5.11.5.1 and 5.11.5.2 Logic (to Avoid Backflow from One Duct to the Other)",
      "equipment_scope": "Dual-duct VAV terminal unit",
      "summary": "If heating air handler is not proven ON, the heating damper shall be closed. If cooling air handler is not proven ON, the cooling damper shall be closed.",

      "required_behavior": "Close the corresponding damper (heating damper if heating air handler not proven ON; cooling damper if cooling air handler not proven ON).",
      "configurable_values": null,

      "title": "Low Airflow Alarm",
      "section_id": "5.11.6.1",
      "section_title": "Low Airflow",
      "equipment_scope": "Dual-duct VAV terminal unit",
      "summary": "If measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 4 alarm. If less than 50% for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 3 alarm. Alarms suppressed for zones with Importance-Multiplier of 0.",
      "trigger_condition": "Measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes (Level 4) or < 50% for 10 minutes (Level 3) while setpoint > 0.",
      "required_behavior": "Generate Level 4 or Level 3 alarm as appropriate. Suppress if zone Importance-Multiplier = 0.",
      "configurable_values": ["70% threshold", "50% threshold", "10 minutes delay", "Importance-Multiplier"],
      "fault_condition": "Measured airflow below threshold for specified duration.",
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm. b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm. c. If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.",
      "page_hint": 98,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "knowledge_type": "fault_diagnostic_rule",
      "title": "Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm",
      "section_id": "5.11.6.2",
      "section_title": "Airflow Sensor Calibration",
      "equipment_scope": "Dual-duct VAV terminal unit",
      "summary": "If the fan serving the zone is off and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.",

      "required_behavior": "Generate Level 3 alarm.",

      "summary": "If damper position is 0% and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the damper is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.",

      "summary": "Provide software switches to force zone airflow setpoint to zero, Vcool-max, Vmin, or Vheat-max; force cooling damper full closed/open; force heating damper full closed/open; and reset request-hours accumulator.",

      "page_hint": 99,

      "title": "Cooling SAT Reset Requests from Dual-Duct VAV Zone",
      "section_id": "5.11.8.1",
      "section_title": "Cooling SAT Reset Requests",
      "equipment_scope": "Dual-duct VAV terminal unit",
      "summary": "If zone temperature exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests. Else if exceeds by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if Cooling Loop > 95%, send 1 request until loop < 85%. Else send 0 requests.",
      "trigger_condition": "Zone temperature relative to cooling setpoint and Cooling Loop output.",
      "required_behavior": "Send 3, 2, 1, or 0 requests as per the hierarchy.",
      "configurable_values": ["3°C (5°F) threshold", "2°C (3°F) threshold", "2 minutes delay", "95% loop threshold", "85% loop threshold"],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests. b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 2 requests. c. Else if the Cooling Loop is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop is less than 85%. d. Else if the Cooling Loop is less than 95%, send 0 requests.",
      "page_hint": 99,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },

Else if < 70% of setpoint while setpoint > 0 and damper > 95% for 1 minute, send 2 requests. Else if damper > 95%, send 1 request until damper < 85%. Else send 0 requests.",

Else if the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint while setpoint is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 2 requests. c. Else if the damper position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the damper position is less than 85%. d. Else if the damper position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.",

Heating maximum is Vheat-max in Occupied/Warmup/Setback, 0 otherwise.",
